question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a consensus timestamp near Long.MAX_VALUE nanoseconds so that `Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, topicMessage.getConsensusTimestamp()).getEpochSecond()` returns a value where `fromSeconds + FILE_CLOSE_INTERVAL_SECS` overflows, causing `DomainUtils.convertToNanosMax` to return Long.MAX_VALUE for `toTimestamp`, resulting in an artificially wide partition overlap query that maps the message to the wrong partition and corrupts the `topic_message_lookup` table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction timed precisely at a partition boundary so that `partition.getTimestampRange().contains(topicMessage.getConsensusTimestamp())` evaluates to false for all partitions, causing the message to be silently dropped from `topic_message_lookup` and making the message permanently unfindable via the lookup index?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: mergeTopicMessageLookup()] Can an unprivileged external user submit two ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions to the same topic with deliberately non-contiguous sequence numbers (e.g., sequence 1 and sequence 1,000,000) so that `cached.getSequenceNumberRange().span(newValue.getSequenceNumberRange())` creates an artificially inflated range `[1, 1000001)`, causing REST API queries against `topic_message_lookup` to return false-positive timestamp ranges for sequence numbers that do not exist?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: mergeTopicMessageLookup()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a high volume of ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions to a single topic across a wide timestamp spread so that `cached.getTimestampRange().span(newValue.getTimestampRange())` continuously expands the stored `timestampRange` in `topic_message_lookup`, causing downstream REST API queries to scan excessively large timestamp ranges and resulting in denial-of-service through query amplification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a consensus timestamp whose nanosecond component causes `Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, topicMessage.getConsensusTimestamp()).getEpochSecond()` to return a negative epoch second, making `fromSeconds % FILE_CLOSE_INTERVAL_SECS` produce a negative remainder in Java, so that `fromSeconds - (seconds % FILE_CLOSE_INTERVAL_SECS)` computes an incorrect window start and routes the lookup to the wrong partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions whose consensus timestamps straddle two partition boundaries within a single `FILE_CLOSE_INTERVAL_SECS` window so that `getOverlappingTimePartitions` returns multiple partitions but the `break` statement after the first match causes only the first matching partition's lookup to be updated, leaving the second partition's `topic_message_lookup` entry permanently missing and making those messages unfindable?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a consensus timestamp that falls in a gap between two time partitions (i.e., no partition's `timestampRange` contains the timestamp) so that the `for` loop in `onTopicMessage` completes without executing the `break`, silently omitting the message from `topic_message_lookup` and causing the message to be invisible to sequence-number-based REST API lookups?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a consensus timestamp of exactly Long.MIN_VALUE nanoseconds so that `Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, Long.MIN_VALUE)` produces an extreme negative instant, causing `seconds % FILE_CLOSE_INTERVAL_SECS` to yield an unexpected negative modulo result and `fromSeconds` to underflow, leading to `DomainUtils.convertToNanosMax` returning Long.MIN_VALUE and corrupting the partition overlap query?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a consensus timestamp of exactly Long.MAX_VALUE nanoseconds so that `TopicMessageLookup.from()` calls `Range.closedOpen(timestamp, timestamp + 1)` where `timestamp + 1` overflows to Long.MIN_VALUE, creating an invalid or inverted range that corrupts the `sequenceNumberRange` or `timestampRange` stored in `topic_message_lookup`?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a sequence number of Long.MAX_VALUE so that `TopicMessageLookup.from()` computes `Range.closedOpen(Long.MAX_VALUE, Long.MAX_VALUE + 1)` where `Long.MAX_VALUE + 1` overflows to Long.MIN_VALUE, creating an inverted range that causes `mergeTopicMessageLookup` to produce a corrupted `sequenceNumberRange` spanning the entire long space?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a large number of ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions to many different topics within a single record file so that `parserContext.merge()` accumulates an unbounded number of `TopicMessageLookup` objects in the in-memory `ParserContext`, exhausting heap memory and causing an out-of-memory denial-of-service in the importer process?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions to a topic with timestamps that cause `fromTimestamp` and `toTimestamp` to be equal (when `FILE_CLOSE_INTERVAL_SECS` rounds down to the same second), so that `getOverlappingTimePartitions` is called with a zero-width range and returns an empty list, causing the message to be silently dropped from `topic_message_lookup`?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions with consensus timestamps that are exactly at the exclusive upper boundary of a partition (i.e., `partition.getTimestampRange().upperEndpoint()`) so that `partition.getTimestampRange().contains()` returns false (since the range is half-open `[from, to)`), causing the message to be dropped from `topic_message_lookup` even though the message exists in the `topic_message` table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions with timestamps that cause the cache key `tableName + '-' + fromTimestamp + '-' + toTimestamp` in `TimePartitionServiceImpl.getOverlappingTimePartitions()` to collide with a different valid cache entry, causing the wrong set of partitions to be returned and routing the lookup entry to an incorrect partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions with timestamps that span across a partition boundary within the same `FILE_CLOSE_INTERVAL_SECS` window, causing `getOverlappingTimePartitions` to return two partitions, but since only the first matching partition is processed (due to `break`), the second partition never gets a `topic_message_lookup` entry, making messages in the second partition unreachable via sequence-number-based queries?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: mergeTopicMessageLookup()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions to a topic with deliberately out-of-order sequence numbers (e.g., first submitting sequence 500, then sequence 1) so that `span()` in `mergeTopicMessageLookup` creates a range `[1, 501)` that includes sequence numbers 2-499 which were never submitted, causing REST API consumers to believe those messages exist and returning incorrect results when querying by sequence number range?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions with a consensus timestamp of 0 nanoseconds so that `Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, 0).getEpochSecond()` returns 0, `fromSeconds` becomes 0, and `DomainUtils.convertToNanosMax(0, 0L)` returns 0, causing `getOverlappingTimePartitions` to be called with `fromTimestamp=0` and `toTimestamp=FILE_CLOSE_INTERVAL_SECS * 1e9`, potentially matching partitions that should not be associated with this message?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions to a topic with a topicId that maps to the same `TopicMessageLookup.Id` (partition + topicId) as another topic's messages, causing `parserContext.merge()` to merge lookup entries from two different topics into a single corrupted entry with an inflated `sequenceNumberRange` and `timestampRange`?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a flood of ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions to a single topic within one record file interval so that `parserContext.merge()` is called thousands of times, each call invoking `mergeTopicMessageLookup` which calls `span()` twice, causing CPU exhaustion and slowing down the importer's record file processing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions with consensus timestamps that cause `fromSeconds + FILE_CLOSE_INTERVAL_SECS` to equal exactly Long.MAX_VALUE / 1e9 seconds, so that `DomainUtils.convertToNanosMax(fromSeconds + FILE_CLOSE_INTERVAL_SECS, 0L)` returns Long.MAX_VALUE, causing `getOverlappingTimePartitions` to be called with `toTimestamp=Long.MAX_VALUE` and potentially matching all future partitions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions with a consensus timestamp that is negative (if the network allows it or if the record file is malformed) so that `Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, negativeNanos)` produces a negative epoch second, causing `seconds % FILE_CLOSE_INTERVAL_SECS` to return a negative value in Java, making `fromSeconds` larger than `seconds` and causing the partition window to be computed incorrectly?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions to a topic that does not yet have a corresponding partition in `topic_message`, so that `isEnabled()` returns true (partitions exist for the table) but `getOverlappingTimePartitions` returns an empty list for the message's timestamp window, causing the message to be silently omitted from `topic_message_lookup` and permanently unfindable via sequence-number-based REST API queries?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions with timestamps that cause the `cacheTimePartitionOverlap` cache in `TimePartitionServiceImpl` to be populated with a stale entry (due to the cache key being based on `fromTimestamp` and `toTimestamp`), so that subsequent messages with the same computed window use the cached (now-stale) partition list and are routed to a partition that no longer exists or has been replaced?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: mergeTopicMessageLookup()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions to a topic with sequence numbers that, after `span()` merging, produce a `sequenceNumberRange` of `[1, Long.MAX_VALUE)`, causing the `COALESCE_RANGE` upsert SQL in `TopicMessageLookup` to store an astronomically large range in the database, making all subsequent `sequence_number_range && $2::int8range` queries in the REST API match this topic for any sequence number query?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/entity/topic/TopicMessageLookupEntityListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onTopicMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions with timestamps precisely at `FILE_CLOSE_INTERVAL_SECS` multiples so that `seconds % FILE_CLOSE_INTERVAL_SECS == 0` and `fromSeconds == seconds`, causing the computed `fromTimestamp` and `toTimestamp` to always align to the same partition boundary, and by doing so, poisoning the `cacheTimePartitionOverlap` cache with entries that prevent correct partition resolution for other users' messages?
